steps.title: Distribute 6x across (x+3)
steps.content:
Apply the distributive property: multiply $6x$ by each term inside the parentheses.
$$6x \cdot x = 6x^2$$
$$6x \cdot 3 = 18x$$

steps.title: Match to the answer choices
steps.content:
The simplified expression is $6x^2 + 18x$.
  • A: $6x^2+3$ — the second term should be $18x$, not the constant $3$. Eliminated.
  • B: $6x^2+18x$ — matches exactly. That matches choice B — B is correct.
  • C: $7x^2+3$ — wrong coefficient on $x^2$ and wrong second term. Eliminated.
  • D: $7x^2+9x$ — wrong coefficient on $x^2$. Eliminated.
